name: Remove duplicates from Sorted array
category: Solve Problems on Arrays [Easy -> Medium -> Hard]
statement:
Given an integer array nums sorted in non-decreasing order, remove all duplicates in-place so that each unique element appears only once.


Return the number of unique elements in the array.


If the number of unique elements be k, then,

  • Change the array nums such that the first k elements of nums contain the unique values in the order that they were present originally.
  • The remaining elements, as well as the size of the array does not matter in terms of correctness.
  • The driver code will assess correctness by printing and checking only the first k elements of the modified array.


An array sorted in non-decreasing order is an array where every element to the right of an element is either equal to or greater in value than that element.
constraints:
• 1 <= nums.length <= 105
  • -104 <= nums[i] <= 104
  • nums is sorted in non-decreasing order.

examples.explanation: Resulting array = [0, 3, 5, 6, _, _] There are 4 distinct elements in nums and the elements marked as _ can have any value.
Resulting array = [-2, 2, 4, 5, _, _, _, _] There are 4 distinct elements in nums and the elements marked as _ can have any value.

nowYourTurn.input: nums = [-30, -30, 0, 0, 10, 20, 30, 30] Select the possible resulting array.
